steps.title: Keep the nation names attached
steps.content: The phrase "Chile and Luxembourg" identifies which fellow OECD nations are being compared. Because those names are essential to the noun phrase, no punctuation should separate them from "nations," and the word "respectively" depends on pairing those names with the two prices.

steps.title: Reject unnecessary punctuation
steps.content: Choice A makes the names look nonessential. Choice B uses a semicolon where there is no independent clause or complex-list break. Choice C uses a colon to introduce what should be a restrictive identifier. The answer is D.
